summary: Iter9 — Admin Watchlist (silent surveillance) feature VERIFIED end-to-end. Backend: 20/20 pytest cases pass + 1 skipped (/users/me is not a GET endpoint, returns 405 — no surveillance leak possible). Covers add (with mandatory reason 400/empty 400/non-admin 403/success 200), idempotent re-add, list shape with target+reason+added_at+added_by, overview gate (403 when target not on watchlist), overview shape (posts/dms/groups/IC/reports/follow counts), DMs both directions captured even when admin couldn't normally see them, silent-to-target invariant on /auth/me + /notifications/counts (no 'watched'/'watch_id'/'under_investigation' keys leak to bob), remove (200 success → subsequent overview 403, list excludes, re-delete 404), and full audit trail with watchlist_add/watchlist_view_overview/watchlist_remove rows carrying admin_id+target_id+at+reason. Iter8 DM-read regression suite (9/9) still green. Frontend Playwright at 1440x900 on https://private-posts-11.preview.emergentagent.com: admin login → /admin → admin-tab-watchlist → add bob with reason 'Verifying report' → toast 'Watching #bob silently · audit logged' shown → watch-row-bob appears → watch-view-bob → /admin/watch/user_bfa838fc3363 → amber 'Silent investigation view. #bob is not notified.' banner visible → all 5 sub-tabs (posts/dms/groups/ic/reports) render with counts → DMs sub-tab shows 12 watch-dm-<id> rows with → (outgoing) and ← (incoming) direction arrows and #alice counterpart handles → watch-unwatch-btn → navigated back to /admin → watch-row-bob gone → regression: admin-tab-reports / admin-tab-csam / admin-tab-audit all still render cleanly. No console errors beyond the expected 2x401 on /auth/me before login.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py:2056 — admin_watch_add idempotent path silently ignores the NEW reason if entry already active. Acceptable (returns note:'already watched') but consider also updating the reason field or appending an audit_events row for the re-add attempt so the paper trail captures repeated escalations. Currently a second add is a no-op without audit entry.

server.py:2110 — posts query limit(100) and dms limit(200) are hard-coded; PRD says dms 'capped 200' which matches. No pagination param — fine for v1 since this is admin investigation, not user-facing feed.
server.py:2114 — dms $or:[{from_id},{to_id}] correctly captures both directions including IC-only conversations the admin couldn't normally see. Verified by TestWatchOverviewDmsBothDirections.

server.py:2024 — is_watched does a per-user query on watchlist. If the watchlist grows large and is_watched is called inside a hot path (e.g., every /feed render), it would become an N+1 perf issue. Currently only called from /admin/watch/{id}/overview so it's fine — but document the constraint so future callers don't bolt it onto the feed pipeline thinking 'one query per request, cheap'.
server.py overview — returns whole user document via {'_id':0, 'password_hash':0}. Good — password_hash explicitly stripped. Verified.

Admin.jsx:223-237 — watch-add-handle/watch-add-reason/watch-add-submit testids match spec exactly. addToWatchlist resolves handle→user_id on the client before POST; this means the admin must know the handle (typo would 404). Acceptable; the empty-state hint covers this.

AdminWatch.jsx:52 — watch-unwatch-btn fires a confirm() dialog (native browser confirm), not a shadcn modal. The PRD says 'confirm modal' — implementation uses window.confirm which is functional but inconsistent with the rest of the app's modal aesthetic. Minor polish, not a bug.
Watchlist remove flow in UI: after unwatch, AdminWatch navigates back to /admin and loadWatched re-runs — confirmed via Playwright the bob row disappears immediately. No stale cache issue.

success_rate.backend: 20/20 watchlist tests passed (1 skipped — /users/me is POST-only, not a leak vector). 9/9 iter8 DM-read regression also passed. 100%.
success_rate.frontend: Add → View → 5 sub-tabs → DM directions → Unwatch → empty list cycle verified via Playwright on the public preview URL. 100%.

test_credentials: admin@clanchat.app/admin123, alice@clanchat.app/Password123!, bob@clanchat.app/Password123! — all working. bob remains role=admin from iter7 but watch endpoints gate on the CALLER's role (admin), not the target's — verified.
seed_data_creation: Test produced 2 transient DMs (bob→alice 'TEST iter9 watch-outgoing-bob' and alice→bob 'TEST iter9 watch-incoming-bob') used by TestWatchOverviewDmsBothDirections. Watchlist entry for bob added then removed inside the same module; module-scoped autouse fixture pre-cleans AND post-cleans. Production sees a clean watchlist after the run — verified via final test_list_excludes_removed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: test_iter9_watchlist.py is the regression suite. Run with REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL exported. Watchlist collection is db.watchlist; audit_events uses event names 'watchlist_add' / 'watchlist_view_overview' / 'watchlist_remove'. Admin.jsx tab id for the Watchlist is 'watchlist' (testid admin-tab-watchlist). Detail page route is /admin/watch/:userId (NOT /admin/watch/:handle — the View button resolves to user_id client-side). Bob's stable id is user_bfa838fc3363, alice's is user_529ecc56114c. Onboarding tour dismissed via localStorage.setItem('onboarding_seen','true'). The /users/me endpoint is POST-only on this codebase (returns 405 to GET) — surveillance-silent test correctly skips it. If a future change adds GET /users/me, the test will auto-validate that no surveillance keys leak.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — feature addition, all acceptance criteria met. No bugs found.
